spec.reasoning: Lexical perturbation: Renamed 'Student Number' to 'Pupil ID' and 'Student Name' to 'Pupil Name' across both sheets — these are equally descriptive full-word names. Renamed 'Data' sheet to 'Records'. I did NOT rename 'Performance' because the instruction uses 'performing' as a general English word ('lowest performing students'), which would risk confusion per Rule 6. Positional title row insert: Added a title row to the Records (formerly Data) sheet, shifting the header row from row 1 to row 2, which tests whether agents hardcode the header position. Distractor column: Added 'Enrollment Year' as a plausible but irrelevant column on the Records sheet to test whether agents incorrectly reference it. No data_value_renames needed since the renamed columns ('Student Number', 'Student Name') don't appear as data values in cells.
